title: Entry plug noise
wikitext:
[]: Entry plug noise             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sat Dec 03, 2011 2:43 am Is there anywhere on the Internets where I can listen to/download the white-noise sound used for the droning hum of the Entry Plug?

[]: Linkman is never coming back at this rate. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Dec 03, 2011 4:28 am This is exactly (not) what you want. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Dec 03, 2011 11:15 am That is actually super relaxing.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sat Dec 03, 2011 3:42 pm I find it a little unsettling personally.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Dec 03, 2011 5:18 pm It seems a bit weird when heard by it's self.

[]: Re: Linkman is never coming back at this rate. Bl             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Mon Dec 05, 2011 1:19 am Alaska Slim wrote: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Linkman is never coming back at this rate. Bl             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Dec 05, 2011 3:02 am Fireand'chutes77 wrote: The second link, that links to the Audiojungle website, sounds the most like the entry-plug noise. It is oddly soothing yet instinctively unsettling at the same time...

[]: Re: Linkman is never coming back at this rate. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Dec 05, 2011 3:07 am ^ That would be because of an expectation of payment. I'm sure if you bought the track, it'd go away.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Dec 05, 2011 3:32 am ^ Oh, so that's how it works! I had thought it was put there as part of the music arrangement. Apparently I did not read the fine details well enough....

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Mon Dec 05, 2011 12:09 pm A link to a sample of the drone in question: http://www.mediafire.com/?l1bvszpbwtel79v Made through several harrowing minutes of effort in WavePad. Pretty shite (there is a click at the start of each loop), but hey, don't look a gift horse in the mouth. If i shouldn't be posting links like this, then my apologies, and please delete the link.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Dec 05, 2011 8:28 pm Looks like that's the best we're gonna get for now. I bet Gainax would demand a couple hundred grand for that sound effect.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Mon Dec 05, 2011 8:34 pm If you're somehow able to edit out the voices, there is a good long chunk of entry-plug sound around 9 minutes and 40 seconds into episode #5 - where Shinji is watching Rei and his father interact on the Cage platform.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed Dec 07, 2011 2:58 am I'm actually kinda curious - do they use the exact same sound effect for every scene, episode and iteration? Are there subtle variations in pitch or whatever in different instances?
